string-functions.label: String Functions (TRIM, UPPER, LOWER)
string-functions.summary: Clean and transform text data with string functions.

sketch0.title: Cleaning and Transforming Text in SQL

sk1.title: Cleaning and Transforming Text in SQL
sk1.bodyMarkdown: SQL provides several string functions to help you clean and standardize text data. The `TRIM` function removes unwanted spaces from the beginning and end of a string. `UPPER` converts all letters in a string to uppercase, while `LOWER` converts them to lowercase. These functions are useful for preparing data for comparison, searching, or display.

q1.title: Purpose of TRIM
q1.prompt: What does the TRIM function do in SQL?
q1.hint: Think about cleaning up extra spaces.

help.concept: The TRIM function is used to remove unwanted spaces from the start and end of a text value.
help.hint_1: TRIM helps when you have extra spaces before or after your text.
help.hint_2: It does not change letter case, just removes leading and trailing spaces.

options.a: Removes spaces from the beginning and end of a string

q2.title: Using UPPER and LOWER
q2.prompt: Which of the following are true about the UPPER and LOWER functions in SQL? (Select all that apply)
q2.hint: Focus on what happens to the letters in the string.

help.concept: UPPER and LOWER are used to change the case of text, making it easier to compare or standardize data.
help.hint_1: UPPER makes text all capital letters, LOWER makes it all small letters.
help.hint_2: Neither function removes spaces, but both help with consistent text formatting.

options.a: UPPER changes all letters in a string to uppercase
options.b: LOWER changes all letters in a string to lowercase
options.c: UPPER removes spaces from a string
options.d: LOWER can help standardize data for comparison

q3.title: Choosing the Right Function
q3.prompt: To make sure 'hello', 'Hello', and 'HELLO' are treated the same in a search, you should use the ____ function.
q3.hint: Think about making all letters the same case.

help.concept: Changing all text to the same case helps with accurate comparisons.
help.hint_1: the missing term and LOWER both change letter case, but which one is commonly used for comparisons?
help.hint_2: Making everything uppercase is a common approach for case-insensitive searches.

q3.template: To make sure 'hello', 'Hello', and 'HELLO' are treated the same in a search, you should use the {{blank}} function.

q4.title: Order of String Cleaning Steps
q4.prompt: Arrange the steps to clean and standardize a text column for comparison.
q4.hint: Think about cleaning spaces before changing case.

help.concept: Cleaning text often involves removing spaces first, then standardizing case, then comparing.
help.hint_1: Start by removing any unwanted spaces.
help.hint_2: After spaces are removed, change the case before comparing.

tokens.t2: Convert all text to lowercase with LOWER

q5.title: Using TRIM and UPPER in SQL
q5.prompt: Write a SQL query to select the 'name' column from the 'customers_cleanup' table, but return the names with no leading/trailing spaces and all letters in uppercase. Name the output column 'clean_name'.
q5.hint: Use both TRIM and UPPER on the 'name' column.

help.concept: You can nest string functions in SQL to clean and transform text in a single step.
help.hint_1: First remove spaces with TRIM, then change to uppercase with UPPER.
help.hint_2: Wrap the TRIM function inside the UPPER function for the 'name' column.
